==Overview== [[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_1gou.jpg|thumb|left|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 1)]] The Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill facing {{ill|Sagami Bay|en|Sagami Bay|WD=Q1061221|lt=Sagami Bay}} near the head of the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|WD=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula}}, and consists of two {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above.<ref>{{Cite web|title=長柄桜山古墳群 (文化遺産オンライン)|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192582|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref>
Around 1994, local archaeologists suspected the presence of a ''kofun'' in this location due to the shape shown on topographical maps, but it was not until trees and overgrowth were cleared in preparation for construction work on a cell phone transmission tower in March 1999, that fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were discovered, and the presence of a tumulus was confirmed. The site is located at an elevation of 127.3 meters and is located the inland side of a hill, approximately one kilometer from the coast. The total length of the tumulus was 90 meters and it was orientated northeast to southwest with the front portion to the southwest. The tumulus was made by carving out the bedrock and then constructing an embankment on the rear and front parts. It was built in three steps for the rear circle and two tiers in the front part. In the center of the rear circle, a seven meter-long depression was found. This was the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], in which a clay-covered wooden casket was placed. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been recovered. Part of the north and east side of the mound have collapsed. Both pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'', as well as {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery shards were found. Further construction on the transmission tower was canceled and the tumulus was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun.
[[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_2gou.jpg|thumb|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 2)]] Shortly thereafter, archaeologists in Kanagawa Prefecture found a second keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' on the same hill about 500 meters to the west, at an elevation of 101.5 meters, facing the coast. The determination was initially made solely by the topography of the site as no ''haniwa'' fragments were initially found, but a preliminary excavation in June 1999 confirmed that it was indeed a ''kofun'', with a total length of 88 meters. This ''kofun'' was orientated east to west, with the front part pacing west, and was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.2 Kofun. ''[[Fukiishi]]'', which were not present on the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun were discovered, and fragments of pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'' were also later discovered. The ''fukiishi'' on the lower part of the mound is mainly yellow {{ill|mudstone|en|mudstone|WD=Q6642424|lt=mudstone}}, and the upper part is mainly white {{ill|Sandstone|en|Sandstone|WD=Q13085|lt=Sandstone}}. The mudstone was carried from the coast because traces of organisms living on the coast can be seen, the sandstone came from the {{ill|Sagami River|en|Sagami River|WD=Q612253|lt=Sagami River}} or the {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|WD=Q1063006|lt=Tama River}}.
Both are estimated to have been built around the 4th century in the early Kofun period. Although the Miura Peninsula appears to be a cul-de-sac, until the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} this area was on the route of the ancient {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō}} highway, and was on the route which the semi-legendary {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} travelled to cross into the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula}} as described in the ''[[Kojiki]]''. The presence of these keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' indicates the existence of a powerful clan in this area, with a strong connection to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kinai area}} in this period of Japanese prehistory.

{{ill|Hayama|WD=Q1342807|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}; {{ill|Zushi|WD=Q230843|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}}| region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1999 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun cluster'''|長柄桜山古墳群|Nagae-Sakurayama kofun-gun}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located on the border of the city of {{ill|Zushi, Kanagawa|en|Zushi, Kanagawa|WD=Q230843|lt=Zushi|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}} and town of {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|en|Hayama, Kanagawa|WD=Q1342807|lt=Hayama|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} in the southern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=長柄桜山古墳群の概要 {{!}} 逗子市|url=https://www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp/syokan/syakyou/newbunkazai/kofun/kofun.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp}}</ref> The pair were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2002.<ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3351|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=国指定史跡ガイド|title=長柄桜山古墳群とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E6%A1%9C%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444603|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumuli in the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|WD=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}}, and the largest in Kanagawa Prefecture.
==Overview== [[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_1gou.jpg|thumb|left|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 1)]] The Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill facing {{ill|Sagami Bay|en|Sagami Bay|WD=Q1061221|lt=Sagami Bay|1=Sagami Bay}} near the head of the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|WD=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}}, and consists of two {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above.<ref>{{Cite web|title=長柄桜山古墳群 (文化遺産オンライン)|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192582|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref>
Around 1994, local archaeologists suspected the presence of a ''kofun'' in this location due to the shape shown on topographical maps, but it was not until trees and overgrowth were cleared in preparation for construction work on a cell phone transmission tower in March 1999, that fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were discovered, and the presence of a tumulus was confirmed. The site is located at an elevation of 127.3 meters and is located the inland side of a hill, approximately one kilometer from the coast. The total length of the tumulus was 90 meters and it was orientated northeast to southwest with the front portion to the southwest. The tumulus was made by carving out the bedrock and then constructing an embankment on the rear and front parts. It was built in three steps for the rear circle and two tiers in the front part. In the center of the rear circle, a seven meter-long depression was found. This was the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], in which a clay-covered wooden casket was placed. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been recovered. Part of the north and east side of the mound have collapsed. Both pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'', as well as {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery shards were found. Further construction on the transmission tower was canceled and the tumulus was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun.
[[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_2gou.jpg|thumb|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 2)]] Shortly thereafter, archaeologists in Kanagawa Prefecture found a second keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' on the same hill about 500 meters to the west, at an elevation of 101.5 meters, facing the coast. The determination was initially made solely by the topography of the site as no ''haniwa'' fragments were initially found, but a preliminary excavation in June 1999 confirmed that it was indeed a ''kofun'', with a total length of 88 meters. This ''kofun'' was orientated east to west, with the front part pacing west, and was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.2 Kofun. ''[[Fukiishi]]'', which were not present on the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun were discovered, and fragments of pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'' were also later discovered. The ''fukiishi'' on the lower part of the mound is mainly yellow {{ill|mudstone|en|mudstone|WD=Q6642424|lt=mudstone|1=Mudstone}}, and the upper part is mainly white {{ill|Sandstone|en|Sandstone|WD=Q13085|lt=Sandstone|1=Sandstone}}. The mudstone was carried from the coast because traces of organisms living on the coast can be seen, the sandstone came from the {{ill|Sagami River|en|Sagami River|WD=Q612253|lt=Sagami River|1=Sagami River}} or the {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|WD=Q1063006|lt=Tama River|1=Tama River}}.
Both are estimated to have been built around the 4th century in the early Kofun period. Although the Miura Peninsula appears to be a cul-de-sac, until the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} this area was on the route of the ancient {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}} highway, and was on the route which the semi-legendary {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} travelled to cross into the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}} as described in the ''[[Kojiki]]''. The presence of these keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' indicates the existence of a powerful clan in this area, with a strong connection to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kinai area|1=Kansai region}} in this period of Japanese prehistory.
Nagara Sakurayama No.1 ;Overall length: 91.3 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 52.1 meter diameter x 7 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 31.5 meters wide x 4 meters high
Nagara Sakurayama No.2 ;Overall length: 88 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 45 meters wide x 8.7 meters high
The site is a five-minute walk from the "Hasakura" bus stop on the [[:en:Keikyū|Keikyū]] bus from {{ill|Zushi Station|en|Zushi Station|WD=Q844325|lt=Zushi Station|1=Zushi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Yokosuka Line|en|Yokosuka Line|WD=Q1332317|lt=Yokosuka Line|1=Yokosuka Line}} and is part of a local hiking course. Hayama Town and Zushi City are currently cooperating to preserve and maintain the tumuli.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|WD=Q6573272|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)}}

{{Coord|35.28652777777778|N|139.58183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Zushi, Kanagawa|en|Zushi, Kanagawa|WD=Q230843|lt=Zushi|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}}-{{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|en|Hayama, Kanagawa|WD=Q1342807|lt=Hayama, Kanagawa|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Hayama|WD=Q1342807|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}; {{ill|Zushi|WD=Q230843|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}}| region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1999 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun cluster'''|長柄桜山古墳群|Nagae-Sakurayama kofun-gun}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located on the border of the city of {{ill|Zushi, Kanagawa|en|Zushi, Kanagawa|WD=Q230843|lt=Zushi|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}} and town of {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|en|Hayama, Kanagawa|WD=Q1342807|lt=Hayama|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} in the southern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=長柄桜山古墳群の概要 {{!}} 逗子市|url=https://www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp/syokan/syakyou/newbunkazai/kofun/kofun.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp}}</ref> The pair were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2002.<ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3351|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=国指定史跡ガイド|title=長柄桜山古墳群とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E6%A1%9C%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444603|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumuli in the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|WD=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}}, and the largest in Kanagawa Prefecture.
==Overview== [[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_1gou.jpg|thumb|left|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 1)]] The Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill facing {{ill|Sagami Bay|en|Sagami Bay|WD=Q1061221|lt=Sagami Bay|1=Sagami Bay}} near the head of the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|WD=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}}, and consists of two {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above.<ref>{{Cite web|title=長柄桜山古墳群 (文化遺産オンライン)|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192582|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref>
Around 1994, local archaeologists suspected the presence of a ''kofun'' in this location due to the shape shown on topographical maps, but it was not until trees and overgrowth were cleared in preparation for construction work on a cell phone transmission tower in March 1999, that fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were discovered, and the presence of a tumulus was confirmed. The site is located at an elevation of 127.3 meters and is located the inland side of a hill, approximately one kilometer from the coast. The total length of the tumulus was 90 meters and it was orientated northeast to southwest with the front portion to the southwest. The tumulus was made by carving out the bedrock and then constructing an embankment on the rear and front parts. It was built in three steps for the rear circle and two tiers in the front part. In the center of the rear circle, a seven meter-long depression was found. This was the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], in which a clay-covered wooden casket was placed. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been recovered. Part of the north and east side of the mound have collapsed. Both pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'', as well as {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery shards were found. Further construction on the transmission tower was canceled and the tumulus was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun.
[[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_2gou.jpg|thumb|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 2)]] Shortly thereafter, archaeologists in Kanagawa Prefecture found a second keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' on the same hill about 500 meters to the west, at an elevation of 101.5 meters, facing the coast. The determination was initially made solely by the topography of the site as no ''haniwa'' fragments were initially found, but a preliminary excavation in June 1999 confirmed that it was indeed a ''kofun'', with a total length of 88 meters. This ''kofun'' was orientated east to west, with the front part pacing west, and was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.2 Kofun. ''[[Fukiishi]]'', which were not present on the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun were discovered, and fragments of pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'' were also later discovered. The ''fukiishi'' on the lower part of the mound is mainly yellow {{ill|mudstone|en|mudstone|WD=Q6642424|lt=mudstone|1=Mudstone}}, and the upper part is mainly white {{ill|Sandstone|en|Sandstone|WD=Q13085|lt=Sandstone|1=Sandstone}}. The mudstone was carried from the coast because traces of organisms living on the coast can be seen, the sandstone came from the {{ill|Sagami River|en|Sagami River|WD=Q612253|lt=Sagami River|1=Sagami River}} or the {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|WD=Q1063006|lt=Tama River|1=Tama River}}.
Both are estimated to have been built around the 4th century in the early Kofun period. Although the Miura Peninsula appears to be a cul-de-sac, until the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} this area was on the route of the ancient {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}} highway, and was on the route which the semi-legendary {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} travelled to cross into the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}} as described in the ''[[Kojiki]]''. The presence of these keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' indicates the existence of a powerful clan in this area, with a strong connection to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kinai area|1=Kansai region}} in this period of Japanese prehistory.
Nagara Sakurayama No.1 ;Overall length: 91.3 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 52.1 meter diameter x 7 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 31.5 meters wide x 4 meters high
Nagara Sakurayama No.2 ;Overall length: 88 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 45 meters wide x 8.7 meters high
The site is a five-minute walk from the "Hasakura" bus stop on the [[:en:Keikyū|Keikyū]] bus from {{ill|Zushi Station|en|Zushi Station|WD=Q844325|lt=Zushi Station|1=Zushi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Yokosuka Line|en|Yokosuka Line|WD=Q1332317|lt=Yokosuka Line|1=Yokosuka Line}} and is part of a local hiking course. Hayama Town and Zushi City are currently cooperating to preserve and maintain the tumuli.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|WD=Q6573272|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)}}

{{Coord|35.28652777777778|N|139.58183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Zushi, Kanagawa|en|Zushi, Kanagawa|WD=Q230843|lt=Zushi|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}}-{{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|en|Hayama, Kanagawa|WD=Q1342807|lt=Hayama, Kanagawa|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Hayama|WD=Q1342807|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}; {{ill|Zushi|WD=Q230843|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}}| region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1999 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun cluster'''|長柄桜山古墳群|Nagae-Sakurayama kofun-gun}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located on the border of the city of {{ill|Zushi, Kanagawa|en|Zushi, Kanagawa|WD=Q230843|lt=Zushi|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}} and town of {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|en|Hayama, Kanagawa|WD=Q1342807|lt=Hayama|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} in the southern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=長柄桜山古墳群の概要 {{!}} 逗子市|url=https://www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp/syokan/syakyou/newbunkazai/kofun/kofun.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp}}</ref> The pair were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2002.<ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3351|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=国指定史跡ガイド|title=長柄桜山古墳群とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E6%A1%9C%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444603|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumuli in the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|WD=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}}, and the largest in Kanagawa Prefecture.
==Overview== [[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_1gou.jpg|thumb|left|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 1)]] The Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill facing {{ill|Sagami Bay|en|Sagami Bay|WD=Q1061221|lt=Sagami Bay|1=Sagami Bay}} near the head of the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|WD=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}}, and consists of two {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above.<ref>{{Cite web|title=長柄桜山古墳群 (文化遺産オンライン)|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192582|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref>
Around 1994, local archaeologists suspected the presence of a ''kofun'' in this location due to the shape shown on topographical maps, but it was not until trees and overgrowth were cleared in preparation for construction work on a cell phone transmission tower in March 1999, that fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were discovered, and the presence of a tumulus was confirmed. The site is located at an elevation of 127.3 meters and is located the inland side of a hill, approximately one kilometer from the coast. The total length of the tumulus was 90 meters and it was orientated northeast to southwest with the front portion to the southwest. The tumulus was made by carving out the bedrock and then constructing an embankment on the rear and front parts. It was built in three steps for the rear circle and two tiers in the front part. In the center of the rear circle, a seven meter-long depression was found. This was the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], in which a clay-covered wooden casket was placed. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been recovered. Part of the north and east side of the mound have collapsed. Both pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'', as well as {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery shards were found. Further construction on the transmission tower was canceled and the tumulus was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun.
[[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_2gou.jpg|thumb|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 2)]] Shortly thereafter, archaeologists in Kanagawa Prefecture found a second keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' on the same hill about 500 meters to the west, at an elevation of 101.5 meters, facing the coast. The determination was initially made solely by the topography of the site as no ''haniwa'' fragments were initially found, but a preliminary excavation in June 1999 confirmed that it was indeed a ''kofun'', with a total length of 88 meters. This ''kofun'' was orientated east to west, with the front part pacing west, and was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.2 Kofun. ''[[Fukiishi]]'', which were not present on the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun were discovered, and fragments of pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'' were also later discovered. The ''fukiishi'' on the lower part of the mound is mainly yellow {{ill|mudstone|en|mudstone|WD=Q6642424|lt=mudstone|1=Mudstone}}, and the upper part is mainly white {{ill|Sandstone|en|Sandstone|WD=Q13085|lt=Sandstone|1=Sandstone}}. The mudstone was carried from the coast because traces of organisms living on the coast can be seen, the sandstone came from the {{ill|Sagami River|en|Sagami River|WD=Q612253|lt=Sagami River|1=Sagami River}} or the {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|WD=Q1063006|lt=Tama River|1=Tama River}}.
Both are estimated to have been built around the 4th century in the early Kofun period. Although the Miura Peninsula appears to be a cul-de-sac, until the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} this area was on the route of the ancient {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}} highway, and was on the route which the semi-legendary {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} travelled to cross into the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}} as described in the ''[[Kojiki]]''. The presence of these keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' indicates the existence of a powerful clan in this area, with a strong connection to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kinai area|1=Kansai region}} in this period of Japanese prehistory.
Nagara Sakurayama No.1 ;Overall length: 91.3 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 52.1 meter diameter x 7 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 31.5 meters wide x 4 meters high
Nagara Sakurayama No.2 ;Overall length: 88 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 45 meters wide x 8.7 meters high
The site is a five-minute walk from the "Hasakura" bus stop on the [[:en:Keikyū|Keikyū]] bus from {{ill|Zushi Station|en|Zushi Station|WD=Q844325|lt=Zushi Station|1=Zushi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Yokosuka Line|en|Yokosuka Line|WD=Q1332317|lt=Yokosuka Line|1=Yokosuka Line}} and is part of a local hiking course. Hayama Town and Zushi City are currently cooperating to preserve and maintain the tumuli.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|WD=Q6573272|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun-gun}} * [https://www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp/syokan/syakyou/newbunkazai/kofun/kofun.html Zushi City Official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.town.hayama.lg.jp/asobi/bunkazai_kyuuseki/5740.html Hayama Town official site] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Coord|35.28652777777778|N|139.58183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Zushi, Kanagawa|en|Zushi, Kanagawa|qid=Q230843|lt=Zushi|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}}-{{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|en|Hayama, Kanagawa|qid=Q1342807|lt=Hayama, Kanagawa|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Hayama|qid=Q1342807|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}; {{ill|Zushi|qid=Q230843|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}}| region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1999 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun cluster'''|長柄桜山古墳群|Nagae-Sakurayama kofun-gun}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located on the border of the city of {{ill|Zushi, Kanagawa|en|Zushi, Kanagawa|qid=Q230843|lt=Zushi|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}} and town of {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|en|Hayama, Kanagawa|qid=Q1342807|lt=Hayama|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} in the southern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=長柄桜山古墳群の概要 {{!}} 逗子市|url=https://www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp/syokan/syakyou/newbunkazai/kofun/kofun.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp}}</ref> The pair were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2002.<ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3351|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=国指定史跡ガイド|title=長柄桜山古墳群とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E6%A1%9C%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444603|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumuli in the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|qid=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}}, and the largest in Kanagawa Prefecture.
==Overview== [[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_1gou.jpg|thumb|left|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 1)]] The Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill facing {{ill|Sagami Bay|en|Sagami Bay|qid=Q1061221|lt=Sagami Bay|1=Sagami Bay}} near the head of the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|qid=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}}, and consists of two {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above.<ref>{{Cite web|title=長柄桜山古墳群 (文化遺産オンライン)|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192582|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref>
Around 1994, local archaeologists suspected the presence of a ''kofun'' in this location due to the shape shown on topographical maps, but it was not until trees and overgrowth were cleared in preparation for construction work on a cell phone transmission tower in March 1999, that fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were discovered, and the presence of a tumulus was confirmed. The site is located at an elevation of 127.3 meters and is located the inland side of a hill, approximately one kilometer from the coast. The total length of the tumulus was 90 meters and it was orientated northeast to southwest with the front portion to the southwest. The tumulus was made by carving out the bedrock and then constructing an embankment on the rear and front parts. It was built in three steps for the rear circle and two tiers in the front part. In the center of the rear circle, a seven meter-long depression was found. This was the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], in which a clay-covered wooden casket was placed. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been recovered. Part of the north and east side of the mound have collapsed. Both pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'', as well as {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery shards were found. Further construction on the transmission tower was canceled and the tumulus was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun.
[[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_2gou.jpg|thumb|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 2)]] Shortly thereafter, archaeologists in Kanagawa Prefecture found a second keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' on the same hill about 500 meters to the west, at an elevation of 101.5 meters, facing the coast. The determination was initially made solely by the topography of the site as no ''haniwa'' fragments were initially found, but a preliminary excavation in June 1999 confirmed that it was indeed a ''kofun'', with a total length of 88 meters. This ''kofun'' was orientated east to west, with the front part pacing west, and was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.2 Kofun. ''[[Fukiishi]]'', which were not present on the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun were discovered, and fragments of pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'' were also later discovered. The ''fukiishi'' on the lower part of the mound is mainly yellow {{ill|mudstone|en|mudstone|qid=Q6642424|lt=mudstone|1=Mudstone}}, and the upper part is mainly white {{ill|Sandstone|en|Sandstone|qid=Q13085|lt=Sandstone|1=Sandstone}}. The mudstone was carried from the coast because traces of organisms living on the coast can be seen, the sandstone came from the {{ill|Sagami River|en|Sagami River|qid=Q612253|lt=Sagami River|1=Sagami River}} or the {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|qid=Q1063006|lt=Tama River|1=Tama River}}.
Both are estimated to have been built around the 4th century in the early Kofun period. Although the Miura Peninsula appears to be a cul-de-sac, until the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} this area was on the route of the ancient {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|qid=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}} highway, and was on the route which the semi-legendary {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} travelled to cross into the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|qid=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}} as described in the ''[[Kojiki]]''. The presence of these keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' indicates the existence of a powerful clan in this area, with a strong connection to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kinai area|1=Kansai region}} in this period of Japanese prehistory.
Nagara Sakurayama No.1 ;Overall length: 91.3 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 52.1 meter diameter x 7 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 31.5 meters wide x 4 meters high
Nagara Sakurayama No.2 ;Overall length: 88 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 45 meters wide x 8.7 meters high
The site is a five-minute walk from the "Hasakura" bus stop on the [[:en:Keikyū|Keikyū]] bus from {{ill|Zushi Station|en|Zushi Station|qid=Q844325|lt=Zushi Station|1=Zushi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Yokosuka Line|en|Yokosuka Line|qid=Q1332317|lt=Yokosuka Line|1=Yokosuka Line}} and is part of a local hiking course. Hayama Town and Zushi City are currently cooperating to preserve and maintain the tumuli.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|qid=Q6573272|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kanagawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun-gun}} * [https://www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp/syokan/syakyou/newbunkazai/kofun/kofun.html Zushi City Official site] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.town.hayama.lg.jp/asobi/bunkazai_kyuuseki/5740.html Hayama Town official site] {{in lang|ja}}

{{ill|Hayama|qid=Q1342807|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}; {{ill|Zushi|qid=Q230843|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}}| region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1999 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun cluster'''|長柄桜山古墳群|Nagae-Sakurayama kofun-gun}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located on the border of the city of {{ill|Zushi, Kanagawa|en|Zushi, Kanagawa|qid=Q230843|lt=Zushi|1=Zushi, Kanagawa}} and town of {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|en|Hayama, Kanagawa|qid=Q1342807|lt=Hayama|1=Hayama, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} in the southern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=長柄桜山古墳群の概要 {{!}} 逗子市|url=https://www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp/syokan/syakyou/newbunkazai/kofun/kofun.html|access-date=2021-07-17|website=www.city.zushi.kanagawa.jp}}</ref> The pair were designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2002.<ref>{{Cite web|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3351|access-date=2021-07-17|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=国指定史跡ガイド|title=長柄桜山古墳群とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%95%B7%E6%9F%84%E6%A1%9C%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4-1444603|access-date=2021-07-17|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref> It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumuli in the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|qid=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}}, and the largest in Kanagawa Prefecture.
==Overview== [[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_1gou.jpg|thumb|left|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 1)]] The Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill facing {{ill|Sagami Bay|en|Sagami Bay|qid=Q1061221|lt=Sagami Bay|1=Sagami Bay}} near the head of the {{ill|Miura Peninsula|en|Miura Peninsula|qid=Q536757|lt=Miura Peninsula|1=Miura Peninsula}}, and consists of two {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above.<ref>{{Cite web|title=長柄桜山古墳群 (文化遺産オンライン)|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192582|access-date=2021-07-17|website=bunka.nii.ac.jp}}</ref>
Around 1994, local archaeologists suspected the presence of a ''kofun'' in this location due to the shape shown on topographical maps, but it was not until trees and overgrowth were cleared in preparation for construction work on a cell phone transmission tower in March 1999, that fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were discovered, and the presence of a tumulus was confirmed. The site is located at an elevation of 127.3 meters and is located the inland side of a hill, approximately one kilometer from the coast. The total length of the tumulus was 90 meters and it was orientated northeast to southwest with the front portion to the southwest. The tumulus was made by carving out the bedrock and then constructing an embankment on the rear and front parts. It was built in three steps for the rear circle and two tiers in the front part. In the center of the rear circle, a seven meter-long depression was found. This was the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], in which a clay-covered wooden casket was placed. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been recovered. Part of the north and east side of the mound have collapsed. Both pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'', as well as {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery shards were found. Further construction on the transmission tower was canceled and the tumulus was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun.
[[File:Nagae-Sakurayama_Kofun-gun_2gou.jpg|thumb|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster (Kofun 2)]] Shortly thereafter, archaeologists in Kanagawa Prefecture found a second keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' on the same hill about 500 meters to the west, at an elevation of 101.5 meters, facing the coast. The determination was initially made solely by the topography of the site as no ''haniwa'' fragments were initially found, but a preliminary excavation in June 1999 confirmed that it was indeed a ''kofun'', with a total length of 88 meters. This ''kofun'' was orientated east to west, with the front part pacing west, and was designated the Nagara Sakurayama No.2 Kofun. ''[[Fukiishi]]'', which were not present on the Nagara Sakurayama No.1 Kofun were discovered, and fragments of pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and cylindrical ''haniwa'' were also later discovered. The ''fukiishi'' on the lower part of the mound is mainly yellow {{ill|mudstone|en|mudstone|qid=Q6642424|lt=mudstone|1=Mudstone}}, and the upper part is mainly white {{ill|Sandstone|en|Sandstone|qid=Q13085|lt=Sandstone|1=Sandstone}}. The mudstone was carried from the coast because traces of organisms living on the coast can be seen, the sandstone came from the {{ill|Sagami River|en|Sagami River|qid=Q612253|lt=Sagami River|1=Sagami River}} or the {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|qid=Q1063006|lt=Tama River|1=Tama River}}.
Both are estimated to have been built around the 4th century in the early Kofun period. Although the Miura Peninsula appears to be a cul-de-sac, until the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} this area was on the route of the ancient {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|qid=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}} highway, and was on the route which the semi-legendary {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} travelled to cross into the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|qid=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}} as described in the ''[[Kojiki]]''. The presence of these keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' indicates the existence of a powerful clan in this area, with a strong connection to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kinai area|1=Kansai region}} in this period of Japanese prehistory.
Nagara Sakurayama No.1 ;Overall length: 91.3 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 52.1 meter diameter x 7 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 31.5 meters wide x 4 meters high
Nagara Sakurayama No.2 ;Overall length: 88 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 54 meter diameter x 7 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 45 meters wide x 8.7 meters high
The site is a five-minute walk from the "Hasakura" bus stop on the [[:en:Keikyū|Keikyū]] bus from {{ill|Zushi Station|en|Zushi Station|qid=Q844325|lt=Zushi Station|1=Zushi Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Yokosuka Line|en|Yokosuka Line|qid=Q1332317|lt=Yokosuka Line|1=Yokosuka Line}} and is part of a local hiking course. Hayama Town and Zushi City are currently cooperating to preserve and maintain the tumuli.
